text: I arrive as busy founder looking for practical upskilling. My past: I need fast practical learning and will leave if the offer sounds abstract.
Today I want to: Check whether there is a clear first step and practical result.
My life goal is: Spend scarce attention only on learning that compounds into better decisions.
I have 100 life-years. Each meaningful action costs 1 month(s); each committed dollar costs 1 year(s).

text: I see "self-degree - AI Learning Platform That Replaces Traditional School". 🇬🇧 AI EDUCATIONAL PLATFORM Stop Spending 10+ Hours Planning Curriculum. Our AI Does It in Minutes. 1 hour here = 4-5 hours of traditional learning. Do the math. I think: This looks aimed at homeschool parents, not a busy founder trying to upskill fast.
My reaction: The promise is bold, but it feels too broad and abstract for my use case.
Idea: Show a sample 7-day practical learning plan with a measurable outcome before the signup button.
Life-cost decision: Spending 1 month of life to continue does not feel justified when the offer is not clearly tied to better founder decisions.
Truth assessment: My honest judgment is that the first step is visible, but the practical result is not specific enough for me. Confidence: high.
